description: End-to-end visualization of the Medical Adherence Predictor ML project — from raw data through model predictions.

panels.title: 💊 Medical Adherence Predictor — End-to-End ML Project

options.content:
## Project Summary
This dashboard visualizes the **Medical Adherence Predictor** — an end-to-end ML project predicting patient medication adherence using Python, R, scikit-learn, and Docker.

| Component | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Dataset** | 2,000 patients · Mendeley Data |
| **Target** | Predict medication adherence (binary: 0 = Non-Adherent, 1 = Adherent) |
| **Pipeline** | Preprocessing → Feature Engineering → SMOTE → Training → Evaluation |
| **Best Model** | Logistic Regression · Accuracy: 55.5% · ROC-AUC: 0.575 |
| **Stack** | Python 3.10 · R 4.x · scikit-learn · pandas · Docker · Grafana |

options.content:
### 🐛 Bugs Found & Fixed

| # | Bug | Location | Impact | Fix |
|---|-----|----------|--------|-----|
| 1 | **Data Leakage** | `preprocessing.py` `scale_features()` | StandardScaler fit on ALL data before train/test split → test stats leaked into training | Scaler now fit on X_train only in `train.py` |
| 2 | **Double Class Balancing** | `train.py` `train_models()` | SMOTE oversampling + `class_weight='balanced'` applied together → over-correction, hurts precision | Removed `class_weight='balanced'` since SMOTE already handles it |
| 3 | **Feature Redundancy** | `train.py` `prepare_features()` | `refill_ratio`, `refill_gap`, `refills_received`, `expected_refills` all present (multicollinearity) | Dropped raw columns — engineered versions used instead |

### 🚀 Improvements Added

| Improvement | Detail |
|-------------|--------|
| **New Model** | Gradient Boosting Classifier — sequential ensemble, best on tabular data |
| **Better RF params** | n_estimators=200, max_depth=8, min_samples_leaf=8 |
| **Better DT params** | min_samples_split=20, min_samples_leaf=10 |
| **Scaler saved** | `scaler.pkl` saved for production inference |

### 📊 Expected Accuracy After Fixes

> The inherent ceiling is ~65–68% due to synthetic data noise. Fixes should push from 55% → **62–66%** range.
> Accuracy above ~68% on this dataset would indicate overfitting.

options.content:
| Layer | Technology | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|
| **Language** | Python 3.10 | ML pipeline, preprocessing, modeling |
| **ML Library** | scikit-learn | Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest |
| **Imbalance** | imbalanced-learn (SMOTE) | Class imbalance correction |
| **Data** | pandas · numpy | Data manipulation & transformation |
| **Visualization** | matplotlib · seaborn | EDA plots (18 figures generated) |
| **Statistical Analysis** | R 4.x · ggplot2 | 8 advanced statistical visualizations |
| **Containerization** | Docker | Reproducible pipeline environment |
| **Reporting** | CSV export | Power BI / Grafana integration |
| **Dataset** | Mendeley Data | 2,000 patient medication records |
| **Dashboard** | Grafana | This monitoring dashboard |

```
medical_adherence_predictor/
├── data/
│   ├── raw/medication_adherence.csv   ← 2,000 patients
│   └── processed/                      ← cleaned, featured, test CSVs
├── src/
│   ├── preprocessing.py               ← encoding, scaling, nulls
│   ├── feature_engineering.py         ← refill_ratio, financial_burden
│   ├── train.py                       ← SMOTE + 3 models trained
│   └── evaluate.py                    ← metrics, confusion matrix, ROC
├── r_scripts/analysis.R               ← 8 ggplot2 analyses
├── notebooks/exploration.ipynb        ← step-by-step walkthrough
├── outputs/
│   ├── figures/     ← 18 PNG plots generated
│   ├── models/      ← 3 saved .pkl files
│   └── reports/     ← final_predictions.csv, model_metrics.csv
├── Dockerfile                         ← containerised pipeline
└── README.md
```
